item.title: Nifty Swings Rise as War Risk Unnerves India Stock Bulls
item.description: Geopolitics, costlier energy and uncertainty over global growth are weighing on sentiment.

item.title: China’s Exports Surge Tops Forecasts as AI Propels Booming Trade
item.description: China’s exports and imports expanded at a faster pace in May, topping forecasts as booming demand for artificial intelligence hardware offsets disruptions from the war in Iran.

item.title: Copper Holds Gain With Easing Iran Tensions, China Data in Focus
item.description: Copper held a gain from the previous session as tensions in the Middle East eased and investors awaited trade data from the world’s top metal consuming country, China.

item.title: World Cup Season Spurs Citi’s Bearish Call on Rates Volatility
item.description: Traders may have another reason to worry less about bond volatility spiking this summer: the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Apple’s iOS 27 Is Filled With Hints About Its Upcoming Foldable iPhone
item.description: Apple Inc.’s iOS 27 and related software updates offer the clearest public signs yet of the company’s upcoming foldable iPhone, revealing references to folding hardware and new features designed for larger, more flexible displays.

item.title: Musk’s xAI Taps Starlink Staffer to Run Grok Training Team
item.description: Elon Musk’s xAI has brought over an executive from SpaceX’s Starlink satellite-internet service to run the team that trains its Grok chatbot, replacing college-aged engineer Diego Pasini, according to an internal note seen by Bloomberg News.

item.title: Fujikura Is Raising Prices on Data Center Cables to Beat Outlook
item.description: Fujikura Ltd. is on a track to beat its outlook thanks to sustained demand for fiber-optic cables essential for AI data centers and a plan to raise prices, according to its top executive.

item.title: Maybank Handles $4.9 Billion Deals for Singapore, Malaysia Hub
item.description: Malayan Banking Bhd said the Johor-Singapore special economic zone will drive further expansion, with Malaysia’s biggest lender having facilitated about 20 billion ringgit ($4.9 billion) in financing and investments in the area in the past couple of years.

item.title: Citadel Securities Expands India Trading and Engineering Teams
item.description: Citadel Securities LLC, the market-making firm founded by billionaire Ken Griffin, is hiring more engineers and traders in India, as it seeks to expand in one of the world’s fastest-growing financial markets.

item.title: GSK in Talks to Buy Nuvalent for More Than $9 Billion: FT
item.description: GSK Plc is in discussions to purchase cancer treatment maker Nuvalent Inc. for between $9 billion and $10 billion, the Financial Times reported, citing unidentified people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Bain Capital Makes Non-Binding Bid for Australia’s oOh!Media
item.description: Bain Capital has joined the pack of bidders for oOh!Media Group, the Australian outdoor advertising company said.

item.title: Katayama Says Japan Undergoing Biggest Budget Reform Since 1945
item.description: Japan is currently undergoing the greatest budgetary reform process since the end of World War II, Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said.

item.title: Australian Consumers Are Deeply Pessimistic as Finances Squeezed
item.description: Australia’s consumer sentiment slipped into “deeply pessimistic” territory as households struggle with cost of living pressures and emerging concerns over the housing market following tax changes.

item.title: Korea Upgrades Economic Growth, Reinforcing BOK Hawkish Shift
item.description: South Korea’s economy grew faster at the start of the year than initially estimated, reinforcing the central bank’s increasingly hawkish stance as an artificial intelligence-led export boom fuels business activity.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Sees LNG Upside Risks as Asian Demand Picks Up
item.description: Liquefied natural gas prices are set to climb to levels not seen in more than three years as hotter weather in Asia and restocking needs in Europe boost demand, according to Morgan Stanley.

item.title: Gold Steadies After Israel and Iran Agree to End Missile Strikes
item.description: Gold was steady after Israel and Iran agreed to end attacks that had jeopardized talks to end the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Sri Lanka Emerging as a New Base for Asia’s Scam Networks
item.description: The crackdown on scam compounds in Cambodia is leading some gangs to relocate to beach resorts and office buildings in Sri Lanka, in what may be the next phase of one of the world’s fastest-growing criminal industries.

item.title: Elon Musk Shows More Detailed Design of AI Data Center Satellite
item.description: SpaceX Chief Executive Officer Elon Musk unveiled a more detailed look at an initial version of an AI data center satellite that the company plans to build, providing more insight into the ambitious project that is driving SpaceX’s highly anticipated IPO set to take place later this week.

item.title: WestJet Slams Canada’s Loan Offer to Airlines Hit by Fuel Costs
item.description: WestJet Airlines Ltd. condemned the Canadian government’s offer of loans to help carriers hit by high jet fuel costs in the wake of the closing of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Asian Stocks Set to Rebound on Iran, AI Optimism: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks are set to rebound from their biggest drop since March, tracking gains on Wall Street, while oil was steady as Iran and Israel pledged to ease strikes that threatened peace talks in the Middle East.

item.title: Oil Steady as Israel and Iran Halt Hostilities That Risked Talks
item.description: Oil steadied after Israel and Iran agreed to end attacks against each other following an escalation of violence that threatened to derail efforts to end the war in the Middle East.

item.title: NY Knicks Success a Win for Lenders Who Propped Up Broadcaster
item.description: Just a year before the New York Knicks rode a double-digit winning streak to the NBA Finals, the company that broadcasts its games was thrown a lifeline to avoid a potential bankruptcy filing. Now, the lenders who backed that restructuring are likely cheering on the team’s success.

item.title: OpenAI Filed Confidentially for IPO as Rivals Race to Market
item.description: OpenAI, the maker of ChatGPT, filed confidentially for an IPO, joining artificial intelligence rivals in tapping public markets to fund ambitious growth plans.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Forces Investors to Bet on Musk’s Entangled AI Empire
item.description: The boundaries between Elon Musk’s companies are growing increasingly blurred through shared capital, talent and infrastructure. Investors must decide what that ecosystem is worth.

item.title: New York’s Penn Station Rehab Eyes Billions in Federal Funding
item.description: The entities transforming New York’s Penn Station are slated to learn how much federal money will be directed to the project in about six months, according to Peter Cipriano, chief executive officer of the Halmar-Skanska consortium, the renovation’s master developer.

item.title: Apple Delays Siri AI for iPhone Users in the EU, Says Regulators Refusing to Engage
item.description: Apple Inc. said it isn’t currently able to launch Siri AI, its redesigned digital assistant, on iPhones or iPads in the European Union, marking the company’s latest standoff with the continent’s antitrust watchdog.

item.title: Apple Downplays Concerns That Its Use of Google AI Models Will Undermine Privacy
item.description: Apple Inc., which just unveiled a revamped artificial intelligence platform built in part with Google technology, said the new approach will still preserve the company’s privacy safeguards.

item.title: First Brands Wins Second Chance to Pursue Liquidation Proposal
item.description: Bankrupt auto-parts maker First Brands can try, for the second time, to win court approval for a creditor vote of a proposal to raise money through lawsuits against the company’s former top officers and lenders, a judge said Monday.

item.title: NZ Commits to First LNG Import Plant Despite Gas Price Surge
item.description: The New Zealand government will take the next step to build the country’s first liquefied natural gas import facility, sticking with its plan to bolster energy security despite the surge in global gas prices due to the Iran war.

item.title: Inflated ‘Private’ Ratings Are Masking Credit Risk, Columbia Study Says
item.description: Ratings that underpin a growing slice of the $1.8 trillion private-credit market, the hottest corner of Wall Street in recent years, are systematically understating investment risk, according to a new study by Columbia Business School researchers.
